title: CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification Exam Objectives
notes: Authoritative source for exam domains, weights, objectives, and scope.

title: Digital Signature Standard
notes: Primary reference for digital signatures.

title: Brute Force Attacks Conducted by Cyber Actors
notes: Distinguishes password spraying and other brute-force activity.

title: Data Confidentiality: Identifying and Protecting Assets Against Data Breaches
notes: Primary guidance that includes tokenization and data masking.

title: Contingency Planning Guide for Federal Information Systems
notes: Primary continuity and recovery planning reference.

title: CSRC Glossary entries for Recovery Point Objective and Recovery Time Objective
notes: Definitions used to distinguish maximum tolerable data loss from restoration time.

title: Digital Identity Guidelines: Authentication and Authenticator Management
notes: Primary reference for authentication factors and authenticators.

title: Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management
notes: Current NIST incident response publication, finalized April 2025.

title: Quantitative Risk Assessment training material
notes: Includes ALE = ARO × SLE and SLE = asset value × exposure factor.

title: Managing the Security of Information Exchanges
notes: Primary reference for agreement types, including service-level agreements.

title: Zero Trust Architecture
notes: Primary reference for Zero Trust principles and resource protection.

title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
notes: Primary control catalog used for security controls, secure defaults, allowlisting, and automation safeguards.

title: Guide for Security-Focused Configuration Management of Information Systems
notes: Primary guidance for baselines, configuration control, change analysis, testing, and rollback planning.

title: Insider Threat Mitigation Guide
notes: Authoritative guidance on defining, detecting, assessing, and managing insider threats.

title: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management Practices for Systems and Organizations
notes: Primary guidance for identifying, assessing, and mitigating cybersecurity supply-chain risk.

title: Phishing Guidance: Stopping the Attack Cycle at Phase One
notes: Authoritative phishing guidance covering attack techniques, user education, reporting, and defensive measures.

title: CWE-367: Time-of-check Time-of-use Race Condition
notes: Authoritative weakness description for TOCTOU race conditions.

title: Application Container Security Guide
notes: Primary guidance on container architecture, shared-kernel implications, images, registries, runtimes, and hosts.

title: Guidelines on Firewalls and Firewall Policy
notes: Primary guidance for firewall placement, policy, segmentation, configuration, and testing.

title: The Transport Layer Security Protocol Version 1.3
notes: Internet Standards Track specification for TLS 1.3 secure communications.

title: Guidelines for Media Sanitization
notes: Current primary guidance for media sanitization, cryptographic erase, verification, reuse, and disposal.

title: Information Security Continuous Monitoring for Federal Information Systems and Organizations
notes: Primary guidance for monitoring strategies, visibility, analysis, reporting, and response to findings.

title: Guide to Computer Security Log Management
notes: Primary guidance for log collection, infrastructure, analysis, storage, and investigation support.

title: Guide to Intrusion Detection and Prevention Systems
notes: Primary guidance distinguishing detection and prevention capabilities and deployment considerations.

title: Technical Guide to Information Security Testing and Assessment
notes: Primary guidance for planning and conducting technical security tests and assessments.

title: The NIST Cybersecurity Framework
notes: Primary governance and cybersecurity risk-management framework, including the GOVERN function.

title: NIST Privacy Framework: A Tool for Improving Privacy Through Enterprise Risk Management
notes: Primary privacy-risk framework supporting data-processing limitation and minimization concepts.
